hooks.command: if [ -n "${SOTP_CLI_BINARY:-}" ]; then "$SOTP_CLI_BINARY" hook dispatch block-direct-git-ops || exit 2; elif [ -x "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/bin/sotp" ]; then "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/bin/sotp" hook dispatch block-direct-git-ops || exit 2; elif command -v sotp >/dev/null 2>&1; then sotp hook dispatch block-direct-git-ops || exit 2; else cargo run --quiet -p cli -- hook dispatch block-direct-git-ops || exit 2; fi

if [ "${SOTP_LOCK_ENABLED:-}" != "1" ]; then exit 0; fi; if [ -n "${SOTP_CLI_BINARY:-}" ]; then "$SOTP_CLI_BINARY" hook dispatch file-lock-acquire --agent "${SOTP_AGENT_ID:-pid-$PPID}" --pid "$PPID" || exit 2; elif [ -x "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/bin/sotp" ]; then "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/bin/sotp" hook dispatch file-lock-acquire --agent "${SOTP_AGENT_ID:-pid-$PPID}" --pid "$PPID" || exit 2; elif command -v sotp >/dev/null 2>&1; then sotp hook dispatch file-lock-acquire --agent "${SOTP_AGENT_ID:-pid-$PPID}" --pid "$PPID" || exit 2; else cargo run --quiet -p cli -- hook dispatch file-lock-acquire --agent "${SOTP_AGENT_ID:-pid-$PPID}" --pid "$PPID" || exit 2; fi

if [ "${SOTP_LOCK_ENABLED:-}" != "1" ]; then exit 0; fi; if [ -n "${SOTP_CLI_BINARY:-}" ]; then "$SOTP_CLI_BINARY" hook dispatch file-lock-release --agent "${SOTP_AGENT_ID:-pid-$PPID}" || { echo "warning: file-lock-release launcher failed" >&2; exit 0; }; elif [ -x "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/bin/sotp" ]; then "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/bin/sotp" hook dispatch file-lock-release --agent "${SOTP_AGENT_ID:-pid-$PPID}" || { echo "warning: file-lock-release launcher failed" >&2; exit 0; }; elif command -v sotp >/dev/null 2>&1; then sotp hook dispatch file-lock-release --agent "${SOTP_AGENT_ID:-pid-$PPID}" || { echo "warning: file-lock-release launcher failed" >&2; exit 0; }; else cargo run --quiet -p cli -- hook dispatch file-lock-release --agent "${SOTP_AGENT_ID:-pid-$PPID}" || { echo "warning: file-lock-release launcher failed" >&2; exit 0; }; fi

echo '{"hookSpecificOutput": {"feedback": "Before going idle: (1) Check the shared task list for pending tasks. If all tasks are complete, verify results. (2) IMPORTANT: Write your work log to .claude/logs/agent-teams/{team-name}/{your-teammate-name}.md. Use available tools (Write or Edit) to create the parent directory first if it does not exist, then write the file. The work log must include: Summary, Tasks Completed, Files Modified, Key Decisions, and Issues Encountered. (3) Report to the team lead."}}'

echo '{"hookSpecificOutput": {"additionalContext": "Context compaction triggered. Key context: Check CLAUDE.md for project rules and workflow. Check track/tech-stack.md for Rust stack. Check track/items/ for current feature specs and plans. Check .claude/docs/DESIGN.md for architecture decisions. Check .claude/rules/ for Rust coding standards. Check project-docs/conventions/README.md for project-specific implementation rules. Run cargo make ci to verify quality before commits."}}'

hooks.prompt:
A Bash command requires user permission: $ARGUMENTS

Analyze the command and decide:

1. Can this be done with a dedicated Claude Code tool instead?
   - File reading (cat, head, tail, less, more) → Use the Read tool
   - File searching (grep, rg, ag) → Use the Grep tool
   - File finding (find, ls, tree) → Use the Glob tool
   - File writing (cat >, echo >) → Use the Write or Edit tool
   - Multiple commands chained with && containing any of the above → Split into separate tool calls

2. If a dedicated tool CAN replace this command, respond with JSON:
   {"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"PermissionRequest","permissionDecision":"deny","permissionDecisionReason":"Use dedicated tool instead","additionalContext":"This Bash command can be replaced by [TOOL_NAME]. Use [SPECIFIC_ALTERNATIVE] instead of Bash."}}

3. If the command genuinely needs Bash (cargo, git, docker, system commands with no dedicated tool equivalent), respond with JSON:
   {"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"PermissionRequest","permissionDecision":"ask","permissionDecisionReason":"No dedicated tool alternative"}}
hooks.statusMessage: Evaluating if dedicated tool can replace Bash command...
